ddls:
CREATE TABLE "posts" (
  "post_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each post */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the post was created */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source platform of the post */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the post */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the post */,
  "user_id" TEXT /* ID of the user who created the post */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the post (if available) */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is verified */,
  "post_url" TEXT /* URL of the post */,
  "media_url" TEXT /* URL of any media associated with the post */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_type" TEXT /* Type of the tag (e.g., hashtag, mention) */,
  "tag_frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the tag across all posts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "post_tags" (
  "post_tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each post-tag relationship */,
  "post_id" TEXT /* ID of the post */,
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tag */,
  "tag_frequency_in_post" INTEGER /* Frequency of the tag within the specific post */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_post_tags_tag_id FOREIGN KEY ("tag_id") REFERENCES tags ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_post_tags_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the user follows */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  "bio" TEXT /* User's bio */,
  "is_private" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user's account is private */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is verified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagements" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "post_id" TEXT /* ID of the post */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes on the post */,
  "share_count" INTEGER /* Number of shares of the post */,
  "comment_count" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the post */,
  "view_count" INTEGER /* Number of views the post has received */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate (calculated as a ratio of likes, shares, and comments to the number of views) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagements_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis record */,
  "post_id" TEXT /* ID of the post */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the post */,
  "sentiment_type" TEXT /* Type of sentiment (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the sentiment analysis */,
  "sentiment_text" TEXT /* Brief text summary of the sentiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the topic */,
  "topic_frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the topic across all posts */,
  "related_topics" TEXT /* List of related topics (in JSON format) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "post_topics" (
  "post_topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each post-topic relationship */,
  "post_id" TEXT /* ID of the post */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic */,
  "topic_frequency_in_post" INTEGER /* Frequency of the topic within the specific post */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_topic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_post_topics_topic_id FOREIGN KEY ("topic_id") REFERENCES topics ("topic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_post_topics_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)
